author: m.kalinin
content:
<@755590043632140352> let's continue here:
> I think we should send that FCU only when proposing

Does that mean no FCUs by attesters?

author: potuz
content: exactly. In the normal scenario it would mean to send FCU only on payload insertions

author: potuz
content: but not simply on slot advance

author: potuz
content: So, **only** send that FCU that causes a rollback if it goes with Payload attributes. This way that FCU is simply a hint to trigger block production more than a rollback

author: potuz
content: yeah this was a bug that was hit by Lighthouse when the first block was empty, the EL fails to propose in that case

author: m.kalinin
content: oh, i see what you mean now. then CL should track rollbacks. Most of ELs are able to propose blocks on top of the head of the chain only, so they have to do a rollback in this case anyway

author: m.kalinin
content: But we don't want to do rollbacks not due to EL incapability but due to UX reasons?

author: potuz
content: I suspect that rollbacks can break applications

author: potuz
content: we **only** send FCU when we import a payload, so that rollback can never happen

author: m.kalinin
content: actually, ePBS rolls back, or any other reorg back to ancestor (honest reorgs also do)

author: m.kalinin
content: we need that rollback to happen when we propose a block, but no dapps should be consuming data from a node serving validator duties, hmm
